config.name: How This Mod Works
config.description: This mod changes the QUALITY of items (Unique / Set / Rare / Magic / Superior), not the QUANTITY. You will not get more drops — the items you do get are more likely to be high quality. Important: many item bases have no Unique or Set variant, so those items silently roll as Rare instead.

config.name: Adjust Rune Drop Distribution
config.description: When enabled, redistributes rune drops toward higher runes. Disable to leave rune distribution at vanilla rates.

config.name: Rune Drop Redistribution
config.description: How aggressively to shift rune drops away from low runes and toward high runes. Does NOT increase the total number of runes you find — the same number of runes drop overall, but the mix shifts. Higher values mean more high runes but fewer low and mid runes.

options.label: Vanilla (1 — no change)
options.description: Original game distribution. Mostly low runes, very rare high runes.

options.label: Light (5)
options.description: Mild redistribution toward higher runes. Low runes still common.

options.label: Moderate (10) — Default
options.description: Zod drops ~10x more often than vanilla. Low runes become noticeably less frequent.

options.label: Heavy (25)
options.description: Strong shift toward high runes. Low runes are rare.

options.label: Maximum (100) — roughly even spread
options.description: All rune tiers drop at roughly similar rates. Low runes become uncommon.

options.label: Custom
options.description: Enter a specific value below. 1 = vanilla, higher = more high runes.

config.name: Custom Rune Redistribution Value
config.description: 1 = vanilla distribution. Higher values shift drops toward high runes at the cost of low runes. Max 100.

config.name: Equipment Item Quality
config.description: Controls the quality tier of dropped equipment. Higher settings mean more items drop as Unique, Set, Rare, etc. instead of Normal. This does NOT affect how many items drop — only their quality. Quality is checked in order: Unique first, then Set, then Rare, then Magic, then Superior — an item uses the first tier it qualifies for.

config.name: Adjust Equipment Quality
config.description: Disable to leave equipment drop quality at vanilla rates.

config.name: Unique Item Chance
config.description: How likely eligible items are to drop as Unique (gold). Vanilla is 0.25% (1-in-400). Note: items with no Unique variant silently become Rare instead. Magic Find has severe diminishing returns for Uniques — even 1000% MF only provides about a 3x improvement. The 10x preset already exceeds what any amount of in-game MF can achieve.

options.label: Vanilla (0.25% — 1-in-400)
options.description: Original game rates. Uniques are very rare — many runs may not produce any.

options.label: 2x (0.5% ≈ 167 MF)
options.description: Roughly equivalent to having 167 permanent Magic Find for Unique items.

options.label: 4x (1% ≈ practical MF cap)
options.description: Roughly equivalent to stacking Magic Find to its practical ceiling (~400 MF).

options.label: 10x (2.5% — beyond max MF)
options.description: Stronger than any Magic Find achievable in-game. About 1-in-40 eligible items will be Unique.

options.label: 20x (5%)
options.description: Very high — 1-in-20 eligible items will be Unique. Expect frequent gold text on the ground.

options.label: Custom
options.description: Enter a specific percentage below. 0.25% is vanilla.

config.name: Custom Unique Chance (%)
config.description: 0.25% is vanilla. Higher = more Unique items. Setting to 0 effectively disables Uniques.

config.name: Set Item Chance
config.description: How likely eligible items are to drop as Set (green). Vanilla is 0.625%. Set is checked after Unique — items that don't roll Unique are then tested for Set.

options.label: Vanilla (0.625%)
options.description: Original game rates.

options.label: 2x (1.25%)
options.description: Twice as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 4x (2.5%)
options.description: Four times as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 10x (6.25%)
options.description: Ten times as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 20x (12.5%)
options.description: Twenty times as likely as vanilla.

options.label: Custom
options.description: Enter a specific percentage below. 0.625% is vanilla.

config.name: Custom Set Chance (%)
config.description: 0.625% is vanilla. Higher = more Set items.

config.name: Rare Item Chance
config.description: How likely eligible items are to drop as Rare (yellow). Vanilla is 1%. Rare is checked after Unique and Set.

options.label: Vanilla (1%)
options.description: Original game rates.

options.label: 2x (2%)
options.description: Twice as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 4x (4%)
options.description: Four times as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 10x (10%)
options.description: Ten times as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 20x (20%)
options.description: Twenty times as likely as vanilla.

options.label: Custom
options.description: Enter a specific percentage below. 1% is vanilla.

config.name: Custom Rare Chance (%)
config.description: 1% is vanilla. Higher = more Rare items.

config.name: Magic Item Chance
config.description: How likely eligible items are to drop as Magic (blue). Vanilla is ~3%. Magic is checked after Unique, Set, and Rare.

options.label: Vanilla (≈3%)
options.description: Original game rates.

options.label: 2x (6%)
options.description: Twice as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 4x (12%)
options.description: Four times as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 10x (30%)
options.description: Ten times as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 20x (60%)
options.description: Twenty times as likely as vanilla. Most drops will be Magic or better.

options.label: Custom
options.description: Enter a specific percentage below. ~3% is vanilla.

config.name: Custom Magic Chance (%)
config.description: ~3% is vanilla. Higher = more Magic items.

config.name: Superior Item Chance
config.description: How likely white items are to drop as Superior (+% Enhanced Damage/Defense). Vanilla is ~8%. Superior items are the best base for runewords. Checked after all other quality tiers.

options.label: Vanilla (≈8%)
options.description: Original game rates.

options.label: 2x (17%)
options.description: Twice as likely as vanilla.

options.label: 4x (33%)
options.description: Four times as likely as vanilla. About 1-in-3 white items will be Superior.

options.label: 10x (83%)
options.description: Ten times as likely as vanilla. Most white items will be Superior.

options.label: Always Superior (100%)
options.description: Every white item that drops will be Superior — ideal for runeword crafting.

options.label: Custom
options.description: Enter a specific percentage below. ~8% is vanilla.

config.name: Custom Superior Chance (%)
config.description: ~8% is vanilla. Higher = more Superior (white) items.

config.name: Advanced Options
config.description: Options that affect how the game's internal drop formula works. The defaults are strongly recommended for most players — only change these if you understand D2's itemratio.txt system.

config.name: Disable Level Scaling
config.description: Recommended: ON. When ON, item quality chances are flat and consistent regardless of monster level — what you set is what you get. When OFF, the game's vanilla formula applies: higher-level monsters produce better quality items. Warning: turning this OFF while keeping Disable Minimum Rarity Floor ON can cause 100% Unique drop rates, because the scaling can reduce the quality divisor all the way to 1.

config.name: Disable Minimum Rarity Floor
config.description: When ON, item quality has no minimum floor — chances scale all the way down to near-0% (effectively removing that quality tier). Only configurable when Disable Level Scaling is also ON; when level scaling is active, the minimum floor is automatically kept in place to prevent 100% Unique drop rates.
